name: BTC 1h Asymmetric Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: continuation is entered after directional reset, while trigger, risk, and exit roles are kept explicitly separate so winners can pay for losers
thesis: Cycle 88 still requires one continuation with asymmetric exit logic and clean role separation, but the 4h versions repeatedly died on sparse or bad triggers. BTC 1h is the better home because it should provide enough recurring continuation attempts to evaluate the management structure without defaulting to pure noise. The edge is role separation on a denser lane, not management rescuing a dead trigger.
invalidation_condition: If BTC 1h continuation still fails to clear density or the asymmetric exits do not preserve positive skew, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price resets, reasserts directional control, and continues, while exit logic gives winners more room than losers without using the same indicator for everything.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Earlier continuation failures mixed trigger, risk, and exit into one muddy stack or asked too much from sparse 4h events. A denser 1h lane with explicit role separation gives the continuation idea a fairer test.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 1h is the best fit because it is active enough for dense continuation tests but usually cleaner than AXS 1h, while still remaining inside the allowed basket.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Continuation may still be too weak in chop, causing the runner to give back the asymmetry the stop was trying to preserve.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If density is fine but winners decay, shorten the time-based de-risk step before touching the trigger.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A moderate number of continuation attempts, many small losses/wins, and a smaller number of larger winners carrying expectancy.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: BTC 1h clears moderate density and the separated-role exit structure outperforms one-shot symmetric exits.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-20 trades, or a profile where the runner never compensates for stop-outs.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable on 1h because reset/continuation events recur regularly enough

test_lanes.why_included: Best-fit 1h lane for role-separated asymmetric continuation testing.
test_lanes.continue_if: BTC 1h shows enough continuation attempts with positive asymmetry from the exit structure.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: BTC 1h continuation is still too weak or too noisy for the asymmetry to matter.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Moderate participation with a few larger winners carrying expectancy.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same role-separated continuation works on the cycle primary lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH 1h preserves the same trigger/risk/exit separation edge.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: ETH 1h proves the concept is too lane-specific.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Slightly noisier but comparable asymmetry profile.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether the same grammar survives when slowed down, even if count falls.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH 4h shows a lower-count but still coherent continuation pattern.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the concept becomes too sparse off 1h.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Lower count, cleaner continuation carry than 1h.

long: Trail remaining 60% by 0.78 * ATR_14 while close remains >= EMA_55
Time-based de-risk: cut 20% after 6 bars if no 0.95 ATR follow-through
short: Trail remaining 60% by 0.78 * ATR_14 while close remains <= EMA_55
Time-based de-risk: cut 20% after 6 bars if no 0.95 ATR follow-through
